name: claude-legal-immigration-laws
description: Shared, venue-independent plugin for U.S. immigration law. Snapshots the canonical rules verbatim — the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA, 8 U.S.C. Chapter 12), the immigration regulations in 8 CFR (DHS/USCIS/CBP/ICE chapter I plus the DOJ/EOIR immigration-court and Board of Immigration Appeals chapter V at 8 CFR 1001-1337), the State Department visa/passport/exchange parts of 22 CFR, and the Foreign Affairs Manual (FAM — 9 FAM 100/302/402/502/504 + 8 FAM 300 + 7 FAM 000/1400) — plus a court-rules corpus for the EOIR immigration courts (the binding 8 CFR Part 1003/1240/1208 regulations, with pointer stubs for the Immigration Court Practice Manual + BIA Practice Manual). As of v0.3.0 it ALSO ships an 11-skill, matter-neutral, document-producing self-help layer that composes with the corpus: immigration-pro-se (forum map + the documents-not-advice / notario-fraud boundary), eoir-immigration-courts (the EOIR system + filing format), immigration-deadlines, immigration-fact-check, eoir-removal-defense, eoir-motions-to-reopen-reconsider, bia-appeals, uscis-benefit-requests, immigration-foia, circuit-petition-for-review, and consular-visa-refusal. Indexes the immigration case-law sources for on-demand lookup: the federal circuit courts (petitions for review of removal orders under INA Sec. 242 / 8 U.S.C. Sec. 1252, via CourtListener and the connected CourtListener MCP), the Board of Immigration Appeals precedent decisions (I&N Dec., via the EOIR Virtual Law Library), and the USCIS Administrative Appeals Office (AAO) decisions. Bundles two free remote MCP servers in .mcp.json so the on-demand case-law layer is wired up out of the box: CourtListener (https://mcp.courtlistener.com/ — Free Law Project's case-law / RECAP-docket / oral-argument / judges database; free account, OAuth sign-in on first use) and Legal Data Hunter (https://legaldatahunter.com/mcp — multi-jurisdictional statutes, case law, and doctrine; free, GitHub/Google sign-in). fam.state.gov omits the Sectigo intermediate from its served TLS chain (the server's own misconfiguration, not a proxy or bot-gate), so scripts/pull_fam.py AIA-chases the intermediate, adds it to a verify bundle, and crawls the site's JSON table-of-contents API for verbatim text. Statutes/regulations are mirrored by scripts/pull_ina.py and scripts/pull_immigration_cfr.py; FAM by scripts/pull_fam.py; the EOIR practice-manual stubs by scripts/pull_eoir_manuals.py; case law stays on-demand per legal-data-apis.md and online-sources.md.
